summary: Magical second set with Saint of Circumstance and soaring Stella Blue
blurb: The first set wanders through familiar acoustic numbers but never quite ignites, while the second set bursts to life with a fiery Saint of Circumstance into Foolish Heart, Lazy River Road and an emotional Stella Blue. Bob’s fuzzy guitar tone and Vince’s solid keyboards shine, and the drum mix is praised as “to die for.” This show is recommended for its standout second‑set jams.
review:
June 16, 1993 at Freedom Hall saw a mixed first set that many listeners describe as “snoresville,” slipping through Cold Rain And Snow, The Same Thing and Peggy‑O without much punch. The real fireworks began in the second set: a soaring Saint Of Circumstance segued into Foolish Heart, then rolled through Lazy River Road before launching into an expansive Corinna jam, a tight Drums/Space interlude, and a heartfelt Stella Blue that sent the audience into a collective hush. Reviewers consistently praised Bob Weir’s gorgeous, fuzzy tone—so forward it becomes a highlight of the tape—and Vince Welnick’s settled keyboard work, which provides a solid foundation without any Hornsby‑style flash. The drum mix was called “trippy” and “to die for,” especially in headphones, while Jerry Garcia delivered aggressive, fast licks reminiscent of ’85 alongside an emotional vocal performance on Stella Blue. Even with a heavy police presence outside, the band’s energy never waned, making the second set a must‑hear snapshot of late‑period Dead magic.

For listeners seeking the clearest window into this night, the Eliason soundboard (gd93-06-16.sbd.eliason.13330.sbeok.shnf) offers an immaculate capture with crisp separation and superb drum mix, bringing out the nuances of the second set’s improvisations. The audience recording also provides a vivid sense of the room’s excitement, but the soundboard remains the definitive source for this performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are positive, highlighting a magical second set with standout jams on Saint Of Circumstance, Foolish Heart and Stella Blue, plus strong band playing. One mixed review offers no criticism. The consistent praise across sources justifies a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, immaculate audio and a superb drum mix that showcases the magical second‑set performance.

key_highlights: Second set magic: Saint Of Circumstance → Foolish Heart → Lazy River Road → Corinna → Stella Blue.
Bob Weir’s gorgeous, fuzzy guitar tone dominates the mix.
Drum mix praised as “to die for,” delivering a trippy second‑set texture.
High energy despite heavy police presence outside the venue.

band_performance.Jerry: Aggressive, fast licks reminiscent of ’85 and emotional vocal/guitar work on Stella Blue; wild‑card shredding fuels the Saint Of Circumstance jam.
band_performance.Bob: Gorgeous, fuzzy psychedelic tone sits upfront in the mix; his vocal feedback adds a spine‑tingling atmosphere during the second set.
band_performance.Brent: Vince provides solid, settled keyboard support without Hornsby’s influence, laying down reliable textures throughout both sets.
band_performance.Mickey: Adds trippy percussion layers; his performance is highlighted as “to die for” in the drum mix, especially during the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in tight with Mickey, delivering a powerful rhythmic foundation that drives the improvisational peaks.
